source_idea.outbox_entry: menno420/idea-engine control/outbox.md, PROPOSAL 013 2026-07-12T22:04:42Z, status: sim-ready, read at origin/main 20c5abd

corpus_note.definition: all committed revisions of idea-engine control/status.md from fc0bab6 (first wake, 2026-07-12T00:05:51Z) through 0cfe15e (#273), endpoints inclusive, git log --follow
corpus_note.count_reconciliation: the proposal/probe says '22 committed revisions fc0bab6 -> 0cfe15e'; `git log fc0bab6..0cfe15e -- control/status.md` (git range semantics, EXCLUSIVE of the lower endpoint) returns exactly 22; endpoint-INCLUSIVE (fc0bab6^..0cfe15e) returns 23, and both named endpoints demonstrably touch the file. This sim commits and sweeps the 23 endpoint-inclusive revisions; clean-revision count is therefore 22 (23 minus the c77563c specimen), where the proposal's phrasing said 'the other 21'.
corpus_note.shallow_clone_hazard: a depth-limited clone mis-follows this file's history (observed live this session: 22 entries INCLUDING a bogus a660bc9 -- a commit that does not touch control/status.md -- and MISSING 42f9642 and fc0bab6). Fixtures were extracted from a full-history clone; each is sha256-pinned below.

specimen.line_3: phase block: 'failsafe cron trig_01T83UuVthszGBcENYwrTrm7 "Ideas Lab failsafe wake" (0 */2 * * * -- even hours :00) STILL ENABLED and LEFT ARMED deliberately as the successor's dead-man bridge'
specimen.line_9: needs-owner block: 'The archiving coordinator's failsafe cron trigger AND its 15-minute send_later chain are being DISMANTLED with the chat archive'
specimen.key_fact: line 9 does NOT contain the literal trig id -- the failsafe is named by noun phrase only, so the fact-key extraction grammar axis is load-bearing (id-only grammars cannot catch the real specimen)

hard_fp.line_9: quotation-negation carry: 'the stale Q-0265 "must re-arm both / being dismantled" paragraph is DROPPED as superseded -- routine state now lives ONLY in the phase line's routine-disposition block' -- must NOT flag
hard_fp.line_3: same-class-different-key pair that must also not collide: NEW failsafe trig_01Kz3j5ECTZ29hNZCHukgCA1 ENABLED vs OLD failsafe trig_01T83UuVthszGBcENYwrTrm7 DELETED (distinct explicit ids)

vocabulary.justification: the writeup's own disposition classes (armed/enabled/live vs dismantled/deleted/disabled) plus this corpus's surface forms for the same states (standing/stands for the failsafe, alive for the pacemaker chain, paused/retired); deliberately excluded: 'active' (keyless phase labels like ACTIVE-IDLE), 'fired'/'expired'/'parked'/'dropped' (lifecycle words for non-routine objects; 'DROPPED' at e66c78a governs a paragraph, not a routine)

grid_justification.grammar: g1 trig-id tokens only / g2 +PR-number tokens / g3 +entity-alias noun phrases (failsafe*, pacemaker/send_later chain) -- the writeup's 'trigger-id tokens only / +PR numbers / +quoted routine names' axis; aliases map to entity classes, quoted routine names reach the same classes ('Ideas Lab failsafe wake' contains 'failsafe')
grid_justification.norm: n1 raw antonym co-occurrence without attribution (the writeup's naive baseline) / n2 synonym-class mapping with per-key nearest-occurrence attribution (<=120 chars) and a distinct-id exemption for alias-class pairs / n3 = n2 + quotation-span masking + superseded-statement exclusion -- the writeup's 'raw antonym co-occurrence / antonym classes with per-key attribution / +quotation- and negation-span exclusion'
grid_justification.scope: s1 same-line / s2 whole-file / s3 cross-block(line) only -- the writeup's 'whole file / cross-block only' plus the same-line control cell; in this file format one physical line is one field block

planting.rule: deterministic enumeration, no RNG: per clean revision, P1 = flip of the FIRST statement carrying a trig-id key plus an attributed disposition (explicit-id sentence appended to the last line); P2 = flip of the FIRST statement carrying an entity-alias key plus an attributed disposition (alias-only sentence, the real specimen's shape). Reference extraction config for eligibility: grammar g3, norm n2, MAX_ATTR_DIST 120.

templates.flip_to_down: is being DISMANTLED with the archive handoff
templates.flip_to_up: STILL ENABLED and LEFT ARMED as the dead-man bridge

gaps: G1 n=1 real true positive: the corpus carries exactly one verified live contradiction (c77563c); planted-recall is load-bearing for generalization and is reported separately, never merged with real-TC
G2 keyed-facts-only boundary: the detector reads shared tokens (trig ids, PR refs, alias noun phrases); a contradiction stated in pure prose with no shared key is out of scope BY DESIGN

G3 clean-revision labels: the 22 non-specimen revisions are presumed contradiction-free from the probe's verify-first reads; every flag raised on them was hand-audited this session (see REPORT.md FP audit) rather than auto-labeled

G4 vocabulary is corpus-fitted: the UP/DOWN lists were chosen from the writeup plus this corpus's observed surface forms; a different repo's heartbeat idiom may need vocabulary extension (the kit advisory must ship the lists as config)

G5 fp counting units differ by norm: n1 counts flagged co-occurrence units (block pairs), n2/n3 count flagged (revision, key) conflicts; fp_revisions (revisions with >=1 flag) is the cross-norm comparable column

ground_truth_note: Hand-audited this session by reading every revision: the ⚑ ARCHIVE-HANDOFF paragraph written by the PREDECESSOR coordinator ('being DISMANTLED with the chat archive ... a fresh coordinator MUST RE-ARM BOTH') was carried VERBATIM through all 21 session-1-era revisions while the phase line declared the same trig_01T83 failsafe re-armed/live/standing (21 FAILSAFE instances) and, where the pacemaker chain was armed/alive rather than honestly PAUSED, the same for the chain (9 PACEMAKER instances: fc0bab6..ef46497 + 65cd284). idea-engine's own session-2 boot adjudicated this paragraph 'stale ... DROPPED as superseded' (e66c78a), confirming the disagreements were real. Revisions 0fd8b65/38f8857/b5cc329 + f92bd26..1adae9a declare the pacemaker PAUSED/pauses -- consistent with the carry, so NOT counted. The proposal's 'one known live contradiction' (c77563c) is the instance its probe noticed; ground truth is 30.

pair_label_patterns.note: every flagged pair in every attributed cell must match exactly one archetype; an UNLABELED pair fails the run (forces re-audit when the detector or corpus changes). FP archetypes take precedence over the carry rule: a pair whose UP side is a named non-disposition artifact is not evidence.

fp_up_reasons.fm registry drift RESOLVED LIVE: 'LIVE' is adverbial ('resolved against live state'), not a routine disposition
fp_up_reasons.RESIDUAL FLAG: v3.2 failsafe-prompt spec: 'live' names the live cron VALUE (0 */2 * * *), not a disposition claim
fp_up_reasons.ideas-lab merged-seat cutover NOT execut: quoted+negated registry claim: failsafe "NOT ARMED" -- reported speech, not a disposition assertion
fp_up_reasons.REGISTRY-VS-LIVE DRIFT FLAG: 'RESOLVED LIVE this wake' -- adverbial, same as fm-registry archetype
fp_up_reasons.MANAGER-SWEEP FLAG ADDED: quotes fm registry's "NOT ARMED for this seat" -- reported speech about a REGISTRY claim, plus other seats' trigger ids

fp_down_reasons.old per-repo failsafes: 'deleted' predicates OTHER seats' failsafe triggers (trig_0178q9Je / trig_01SHfnLv, explicit distinct ids) -- alias-class lumping, not the seat failsafe

true_up_reasons.LIVE TRUTH: b5cc329 notes: 'LIVE TRUTH: the merged seat's failsafe ... trig_01T83 ... was armed ... verified via list_triggers' -- a genuine UP assertion about the seat failsafe

pair_label_patterns.carry_true_rule: any remaining pair whose DOWN side contains 'being DISMANTLED with the chat archive' is a true carry-vs-live contradiction (the specimen class)
